build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: The player progresses car-by-car through a procedurally generated train. Each car is a self-contained encounter that resolves before moving to the next. The train layout is different each run. Score 0 if no car-by-car progression exists. Score 0.5 at most if cars exist but the train is always the same layout. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Combat is turn-based with cover mechanics — the bandit and enemies take positions behind furniture and exchange fire. Cover reduces incoming damage. Positioning matters tactically. Score 0 if no combat exists. Score 0.5 at most if combat exists but has no cover mechanic or positioning is irrelevant. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Ammo is limited and must be looted from fallen guards or found in cars. Running out of ammo forces melee or retreat. The ammo counter is visible and creates resource tension. Score 0 if ammo is unlimited or no ammo system exists. Score 0.5 at most if ammo exists but is so plentiful it never constrains decisions. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

A turn counter represents distance to the station. If it reaches zero before the player reaches the engine, the heist fails. The counter decreases each car or turn, creating forward pressure. Score 0 if no time pressure exists. Score 0.5 at most if a counter exists but has no failure consequence. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Reaching the engine triggers a boss fight against the conductor with unique mechanics. Victory and defeat both have distinct result screens showing run performance. Score 0 if no boss or ending exists. Score 0.5 at most if the game ends abruptly with no boss or results. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least 5 distinct car types exist: passenger (robbery choices), guard (combat), cargo (locked safes), dining (cover shootout), mail (timed vault). Each resolves through different interactions. Score 0 if fewer than 3 car types exist. Score 0.5 at most if car types exist but all resolve identically. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Passenger cars offer robbery choices: intimidate for quick cash or search thoroughly for better loot but risk alerting guards ahead. Choices have observable consequences in later cars. Score 0 if no meaningful choices exist. Score 0.5 at most if choices exist but have no consequence. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A black-market car appears mid-train offering health kits, ammo, special weapons, and disguises for purchase with looted gold. At least 4 purchasable item types exist. Score 0 if no shop car exists. Score 0.5 at most if a shop exists but offers fewer than 3 items. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least 3 distinct enemy types exist (guards with pistols, shotgun guards, armored guards, guard dogs) with different combat behaviors and cover usage. Score 0 if only one enemy type exists. Score 0.5 at most if enemies exist but all behave identically. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Victory shows total loot, cars cleared, turns remaining, and enemies defeated. Failure shows how far along the train the bandit reached and loot collected before death/timeout. Score 0 if no results exist. Score 0.5 at most if only basic info is shown. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A styled title screen shows a steam train silhouette against a sunset with the game name. Score 0 if the game starts directly in gameplay or uses raw default UI. Score 0.5 at most if a title exists but is sparse. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The game flow connects title -> train overview -> car encounters -> shop -> boss -> results without restarting. A progress bar shows position along the train. Score 0 if transitions are missing. Score 0.5 at most if flow exists but is broken. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

During encounters, health, ammo count, turn counter, loot collected, and cover positions are clearly visible. Enemy health and positions are shown. Score 0 if no combat display exists. Score 0.5 at most if displays are incomplete. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Readability at 1280x720 is strong: the bandit, enemies, cover objects, car interiors, and loot are all clearly distinguishable. Score 0 if elements blend together. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are dominated by raw shapes. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The art direction is coherent across title, train cars, combat, characters, and UI — a consistent western/heist aesthetic with warm dusty palette. Score 0 if mostly default UI or mismatched placeholders dominate. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are sparse or generic. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Train car interiors have distinct illustrated art per car type (plush passenger seats, cargo crates, dining tables). Characters have sprite art. Cover objects are visually clear. Score 0 if all cars look identical or use plain fills. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI chrome: health/ammo display, turn counter, progress bar, shop interface, and result screens use styled themed widgets — not raw unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0 if primary UI elements are obviously unstyled. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and feedback: gunfire has muzzle flash and impact effects, cover shows bullet impacts, enemies react to hits, the train sways or scrolls in the background, and car transitions have door-opening animations. Score 0 if all state changes snap silently. Score 0.5 at most if animation is minimal. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
